video_analysis.attire_explanation: The doctor is wearing a white clinical coat, visible in all frames.
video_analysis.visibility_explanation: Both the doctor (right side) and the customer (left side) are clearly visible in all provided frames.
video_analysis.privacy_explanation: The doctor's background appears to be a plain, light-colored wall or curtain, with no visible private information, people, or confidential data. Privacy is maintained.

qa_matrix.question_text: Kindly confirm your full Name
Your Date of birth
Education Qualification
Your current Occupation
Please show your ID proof
Kindly share your current location( City and state with country)?

You Divyarup Ghosh Dastidar , declare that, you have fully understood the questions asked to you as per this call and have furnished complete, true and accurate information after fully understanding the same. Sir/Mam' are you in agreement with the declaration that I have read out to you.
In the last 24 months, have you smoked or used any tobacco or nicotine products?
Do you consume alcohol ?
Please select the type of alcohol you consume.
In a week,How many units do you consume ? 1 unit of alcohol = 1 bottle of beer (350 ml)
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: The doctor did not ask about the number of units of alcohol consumed per week, only the frequency (occasional). The MER has '1' unit.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you ever been advised by a doctor to reduce or stop alcohol consumption ?
Have you ever used recreational drugs or Narcotics that was not prescribed by a Licensed physician ?
What is Proposed insured height (cm)?
What is Proposed Insured weight (kg)?
High/Low blood pressure, raised cholesterol or triglycerides or lipids

Chest pain or heaviness or discomfort, heart murmur, heart attack, coronary artery disease, Heart valve disorder, congenital heart disease, palpitation, rheumatic fever, shortness of breath or any other heart conditions
Cancer or carcinoma-in-situ, pre-cancerous condition, leukaemia, enlarged nodes, unusual skin lesions, dysplasia, leukoplakia, sacroma, tumours, polyps, nodules, cysts, lump or other growths
Diabetes, raised blood sugar or sugar in urine,Hyperthyroidism, Hypothyroidism , other thyroid disorders, or any other endocrine disorders
Asthma, bronchitis, sinusitis, tuberculosis, pneumonia, chronic or recurrent cough, sleep apnea, emphysema, chronic pulmonary disease (COPD) or any other respiratory disorders
Hepatitis, hepatitis carrier, fatty liver, liver cirrhosis, jaundice, alcoholic liver disease or any other diseases or disorders of the liver
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yes, ma'am. Yeah. So, I did a USG two years back where it was seen that I have fatty liver one, grade one.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you undergone any biopsy for this condition?
Have you been advised by your doctor to undergo biopsy?
Have you been advised by your doctor that your condition is caused by alcohol drinking?
Are you on treatment prescribed by your doctor?
Was there any liver USG done in the past 6 months?
Anemia, thalassemia, systemic lupus erythematosus or any other diseases or disorders of the blood or autoimmune disease

Anxiety, depression, epilepsy, paralysis, weakness of limb, stroke, transient ischemic attack (TIA), multiple sclerosis, Parkinson's disease, Dementia, Alzheimer's or any other neurological, nervous, mental health disorders?

Gastritis, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), stomach ulcer, recurrent indigestion, irritable bowel syndrome, Barrett's esophagus, atrophic gastritis, Crohn's disease, ulcera-tive colitis, Pancreatitis/Pancreatic disorder or any gastrointestinal diseases or disorders?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Uh, two years back I had gallbladder removal surgery.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer disclosed gallbladder removal surgery due to gallstones. This should have been captured under gastrointestinal diseases, but the doctor asked to mention it in the next question.

qa_matrix.question_text: Kidney stones, gall stones, kidney or urinary tract infection, blood or protein in urine, chronic kidney disease, kidney failure, piles, fistula, hydrocele or any other disorders of the kidney, bladder, genital, prostate or urinary systems?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Uh, yes. Uh, I had a gallbladder removal surgery on February 26, 2024. ... Gallstone was found. One gallstone was found, after which it was removed.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you fully recovered with no further symptoms, investigations, treatment or compli-cations?
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer stated 'No' to ongoing symptoms, but MER recorded 'Yes' for full recovery.

qa_matrix.question_text: When you have recovered. Please confirm duration in months
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: The doctor did not ask about the duration of recovery.

qa_matrix.question_text: Recurrent Back pain, arthritis, gout, slipped disc, muscular dystrophies, deformity, physical disability or any other disorders of muscle, bones, joints, limbs or spine?
Blindness,cataracts, glaucoma, impaired sight, double vision or any other diseases or disorders of eyes
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, no, ma'am. I only wear spectacles because I have uh... eyesight. Yeah. Far objects. Minus 2.25 power.

qa_matrix.question_text: Deafness, Impaired hearing, speech problem, hoarseness of voice, difficult in swal-lowing, ear discharge or any other diseases or disorders of ears, nose or throat

Is there anything else that you would like to share with us with respect to your health/ habits or anything else that you believe is important to ensure that policy is processed basis all facts & information?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Just one thing. I have been applying medicine called Morf 5% for hair fall. ... I apply it on my scalp. I have been doing it for the last five years, from roughly 2020.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Customer disclosed using Morf 5% for hair fall since 2020, but MER recorded 'No'.

qa_matrix.question_text: Other than what you have already told us, have you had a medical condition that has required any of the following within the last 5 years a)Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or pre-scribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endo-scopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test
qa_matrix.captured_response: No, nothing much. Uh there was one instance in endoscopy, so before my surgery, all the tests were done. So an endoscopy was also performed. So during that, um in the stomach, there was some uh something which was found. ... was found. Uh so a biopsy was done for that, and there was no abnormal finding of that biopsy report.

qa_matrix.question_text: Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consec-utive weeks at a time
Have you suffered from any congenital defect or disorder or hereditary disease such as cerebral palsy, Down's syndrome, poliomyelitis
Have you or your spouse or partner been told to undergo tests or treatment, or received any medical advice or counselling in connection with sexually transmitted diseases, including HIV and AIDS?

Apart from what you have disclosed, are you experiencing any unexplained weight loss/gain or any sign/symptoms that you are likely to seek medical advice or treatment for or awaiting any surgical procedure.

critical_issues: The doctor failed to ask about the number of alcohol units consumed per week, which is a specific question in the MER.
The MER incorrectly states 'Yes' for full recovery from cholecystectomy, despite the customer not confirming ongoing symptoms (answered 'No' to symptoms).
The MER incorrectly recorded 'No' for additional health information, even though the customer disclosed using Morf 5% for hair fall.
The doctor misdirected the customer to discuss gallbladder surgery under kidney/urinary disorders instead of gastrointestinal diseases.
recommendations: The doctor should ensure all specific sub-questions from the MER, especially quantitative ones like alcohol units and recovery duration, are explicitly asked.
All health disclosures made by the customer, even if not directly prompted, must be accurately captured and documented in the MER.
The doctor should ensure medical conditions are correctly mapped to the appropriate body systems during the interview to avoid miscategorization.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative and responsive throughout the call.
Customer provides detailed answers when prompted for clarification.

meta.insurance_company: Tata AIA Life Insurance Company Ltd.

video_analysis.attire_explanation: The doctor is wearing a white clinical coat, visible in all frames.
video_analysis.visibility_explanation: Both the doctor (right side) and the customer (left side) are clearly visible in all provided frames.
video_analysis.privacy_explanation: The doctor's background appears to be a plain, light-colored wall or curtain, with no visible private information, people, or confidential data. Privacy is maintained.

greetings.explanation: The doctor greeted the customer at the beginning of the call.

call_opening.explanation: The doctor introduced herself, stated the company name, and confirmed the client's name.

language_preference.explanation: The doctor asked for the customer's primary mother language, not their preferred language for the call.

id_validation.explanation: The doctor asked the customer to show ID proof and confirm the last four digits of their Aadhaar card.

disclaimer.explanation: The doctor informed the customer about call recording and the legal implications of providing incorrect information.

politeness.explanation: The doctor consistently used polite language such as 'sir', 'ma'am', 'kindly', and 'please' throughout the call.

empathy.explanation: The customer disclosed medical conditions, but no serious health concerns requiring explicit empathy were presented with distress.

communication_skills.explanation: The doctor generally communicated clearly and rephrased questions. However, there were a couple of instances of incomplete sentences or cut-off questions.

probing.explanation: The doctor effectively probed into all disclosed medical conditions (fatty liver, gallbladder surgery, eyesight, hair fall medicine, endoscopy findings) to gather necessary details.

observations.explanation: The customer reported a video visibility issue with the doctor during the call, which was an unusual event.

call_closure.explanation: The doctor took a final declaration from the customer and thanked them for their cooperation.
